reasoning: The men's 50m backstroke long course world record is currently 23.55, held by Kliment Kolesnikov since July 2023. World records in long course swimming are rarely broken outside of major summer international competitions or spring national trials. The winter months (December to February) typically feature short course competitions or early-season training meets where long course world records are extremely uncommon. There are no pending world records in this event according to recent searches. Furthermore, even if Kolesnikov himself breaks his own record, the resolution remains YES since he would still hold the record. The only way it resolves NO is if another swimmer surpasses his mark. Given the lack of major long course events between late December 2025 and March 2026, the probability of someone else breaking this record is very low.
